 
August 14, 2023 
 
RE: Docket No. CFPB–2023–0038 
 
Dear Director Chopra, 
 
I respectfully submit this letter on behalf of the American Association of Dental Office 
Management (AADOM). AADOM is the nation’s largest professional association for dental office 
managers and practice administrators. We have worked with CareCredit for many years. 
CareCredit’s access to care aligns with AADOM’s values.  
 
The ability of patients to access and receive quality dental care is important to AADOM and our 
members. Over my 20 years as president of AADOM, I have worked with innumerable dental 
office managers and practice administrators who have responsibly utilized CareCredit in their 
practices and they are grateful it has been an option for patients to access care they otherwise could 
not.  
 
Patients have out-of-pocket expenses for medical care, including dentistry, and not all patients 
have sufficient insurance or cash on hand to pay for the dental work they want or need at the time 
of service.  
 
So many dentists are true caregivers at their core, business owners or business associates 
secondarily. Dental practices must remain profitable to remain in business to continue to provide 
necessary healthcare. To that end, offering payment options such as CareCredit is both responsible 
and necessary.  
 
I urge your committee to reconsider your stance on practical, responsible credit options for patients 
and their medical/dental needs.  
 
Sincerely, 
 
Heather Colicchio 
Heather Colicchio 
Founder and President
